metaTitle: Bcrypt Explained — Slow Hashing for Passwords
metaDescription: Learn why bcrypt is intentionally slow, when to use it, and why password hashing is different from encryption.

quick.what: Hashes passwords so you can verify them later without storing the original password.
quick.use: Protecting password databases and authentication systems.
quick.notFor: Reversible encryption, file secrecy, or token generation.

intro.title: In plain English
intro.text: Bcrypt is a password-specific hashing algorithm. It turns a password into a one-way result that can be checked later, but not turned back into the original password.

how.title: How it works
how.text: Bcrypt mixes the password with a random salt and repeats expensive work many times. That makes each guess slower for an attacker, which is exactly what you want for password protection.

useCases.title: Where you'd use it
items: Storing user passwords in a web application database.
Testing password verification logic during development.
Migrating away from unsafe fast hashes for login systems.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: People sometimes ask bcrypt to protect data they want to decrypt later. That is the wrong tool. Bcrypt is for checking passwords, not for getting the original secret back.

history.title: History / fun fact
history.text: Bcrypt was designed to stay expensive on purpose. In password security, slow is a feature because it makes brute-force guessing much harder.

security.title: Security note
security.text: The work factor must be tuned high enough to hurt attackers but still be acceptable for your own login performance.

sections.title: Why salts matter
sections.text: A salt makes the same password produce different hashes for different users. That breaks simple precomputed lookup attacks like rainbow tables.

sections.title: Why cost factor matters
sections.text: The cost factor controls how much work bcrypt performs. Raising it increases defense against guessing, but also increases legitimate verification time.

diagram.title: Password in, slow hash out
diagram.caption: Bcrypt adds salt and cost so password checking stays practical while mass guessing stays expensive.
